question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated requests with incrementing tokenIds bounds to perform a binary search over the token ID space, enumerating all tokens with PENDING airdrops and their associated fee amounts (stored in the amount field), reconstructing the complete fee schedule outside design parameters without any rate limiting?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Bound constructor only validates adjustedLower > adjustedUpper for primarySortField=true, by providing an inverted range on the serialNumbers Bound (primarySortField=false), causing the jOOQ query to execute with a WHERE SERIAL_NUMBER BETWEEN high AND low condition that returns zero results, masking fee records that should be visible outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an accountId using an EVM address format that resolves to a different EntityId than the shard.realm.num format for the same account, causing entityService.lookup() to return a different entity ID and the query to return airdrop fee records belonging to a different account outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cardinality tracking in Bound (using Math.addExact in cardinality.merge()) to cause an ArithmeticException by providing more than Integer.MAX_VALUE range parameters of the same operator type, causing a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate fee record queries from completing outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a tokenIds Bound with operator GTE and value 0 combined with a serialNumbers Bound with operator LTE and value Long.MAX_VALUE to effectively remove all secondary filters from the query, causing findAll() to return all PENDING airdrop records for an account regardless of token or serial constraints, exposing all fee amounts outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getBounds() method where if entityIds is empty and tokenIds is empty, only serialNumbers is returned as the sole bound, causing the jOOQ query to filter only by serial number without any token ID constraint, returning airdrop fee records across all tokens for the given account outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a negative value for the limit parameter (bypassing the @Positive constraint via HTTP parameter injection or type confusion) to cause the jOOQ LIMIT clause to use a negative value, potentially causing the database to return all matching records and exposing all airdrop fee amounts outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the toLower() and toUpper() methods in Bound by triggering them on a Bound with null lower or upper respectively, causing createBound() to return Bound.EMPTY, and then using this empty bound in a chained request to bypass fee-related range filters in the jOOQ query outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ImmutableSortedMap in the EXTRACTOR function of TokenAirdropsController, which sorts keys alphabetically, to observe that the pagination cursor always includes receiver_id, sender_id, serial_number, and token_id, and use this to craft a next-page link that skips over airdrop records with specific fee amounts, causing fee data gaps outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a tokenIds Bound with a single EQ operator value that matches a token with a custom fee schedule, and by observing whether the response includes that token's airdrop records, determine whether a specific custom fee schedule is active, enabling fee schedule probing outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Bound.of() method for TimestampParameter rewrites EQ operators to LTE, and if a similar rewrite is applied to EntityIdRangeParameter, cause the query to use a less-than-or-equal condition instead of equality, returning airdrop records with fee amounts for all tokens up to the specified ID outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a serialNumbers Bound with both a lower and upper bound where hasEqualBounds() returns true (adjustedLower == adjustedUpper), causing adjustLowerRange() to rewrite the bound to EQ and set upper=null, and then exploit the null upper in subsequent processing to bypass the serial number range filter and return airdrop fee records outside the intended serial range?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @NullUnmarked annotation on the Bound class to pass null values for the field parameter, causing a NullPointerException in the jOOQ query builder that leaks stack trace information revealing internal fee-related field names and query structure outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the SORT_ORDERS map uses TOKEN_AIRDROP.SERIAL_NUMBER.asc() as the tertiary sort field, and by providing a serialNumbers Bound that filters to a single serial number, cause the query to return airdrop records in an order that reveals the fee amounts of NFT airdrops in a predictable sequence outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an accountId in the path variable that is a valid alias for multiple accounts (due to alias collision), causing entityService.lookup() to resolve to an unintended account ID, and the query to return airdrop fee records belonging to a different account outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getBounds() method's conditional logic where if entityIds is non-empty but tokenIds is empty, only entityIds is returned as the primary bound with no secondary bound, causing the jOOQ query to omit the TOKEN_ID filter entirely and return all airdrop fee records for any token associated with the specified receiver/sender outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a tokenIds Bound with operator GT and value 0 to return all token IDs greater than 0 (effectively all tokens), combined with limit=MAX_LIMIT, to retrieve the maximum number of airdrop records with their fee amounts in a single request, enabling bulk fee data harvesting outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the verifyUnsupported() method in Bound by providing an operator that is explicitly marked as unsupported, causing an IllegalArgumentException whose message includes the parameterName constant (e.g., RECEIVER_ID, SENDER_ID), leaking internal API parameter names that can be used to craft fee-targeted queries outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Bound constructor processes params in array order, where the last GT/GTE parameter overwrites the lower field and the last LT/LTE parameter overwrites the upper field, by providing two GT parameters to silently discard the first lower bound and use only the second, causing the query to use an unintended lower bound on fee-related fields outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a tokenIds Bound with a LT operator and value 1 to cause adjustUpperBound() to return 0, and since token IDs start at 1, the query returns zero results, effectively hiding all airdrop fee records for the account and causing the API to report no pending fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getSort() method in TokenAirdropsController where airdrops.isEmpty() causes the sort to default to Sort.by(order, primarySortField, TOKEN_ID, SERIAL_NUMBER), and by crafting a request that returns an empty first page, force the pagination link to use a sort order that skips fee records on subsequent pages outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Bound class uses EnumMap<RangeOperator, Integer> for cardinality tracking, and by providing a mix of GT and GTE operators (which are tracked separately), bypass the verifySingleOccurrence() check that groups them together, causing the query to use both a GT and GTE lower bound simultaneously with undefined behavior on fee record filtering outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a serialNumbers Bound with operator GTE and value Long.MIN_VALUE to cause getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() to return Long.MIN_VALUE (since GTE does not increment), effectively setting the lower bound to the minimum long value and matching all serial numbers including negative values that may represent special fee-exempt NFT categories outside design parameters?
